title: Authentication, Authorization, Tenant Isolation, and Privacy
description: User IDs arrive in headers, handlers authorize by route presence, shared source data leaks private follows, audit logs capture tokens and reading history, and deletion scope is unclear. Learners cumulatively produce tenant-safe identity and privacy boundary through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Verify configured token issuer, audience, algorithm, time, key rotation, and transport before constructing a minimal principal.
Authorize each command from principal, tenant, role, ownership, relationship, and current resource state inside the application boundary.
Require tenant scope in repository APIs, SQL predicates, unique keys, tests, metrics, exports, and administrative paths.
